channel.title: New local problems on FixMyStreet
channel.description: The latest local problems reported by users

item.title: Outside 151 and 151A York way, 15th March
item.description:
This pothole was previously filled as it was cashing looking of water and slashing my property door as well as people exiting the property. Despite the metal drain being filled with asphalt the diver and pothole is still in the road. Further more as the metal drain was asphalted over it makes a mound metalic thud and vibration through my flat especially my bedroom everytime a car drives over it. Current issues: - very loud thud noise as cars and HGVs drive over it. This keeps us awake throughout the night. - water splashing me and my family. -water causing irreparable damage to my property as the wood is now rotten. I have followed the appropriate process throughout the year and have raked this numeres times. However the council have not addressed the issue properly or within a reasonable time despite follow ups. I have also mentioned that I am unwell and this issue is causing significant alarm and distress. Under the Highways Act 1980 the council has a duty to maintain the highway. If the issue cannot be addressed within a reasonable timeframe, I may need to consider further action regarding the damage and disruption being caused.
Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): York Way, Holloway, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Please remove asap, 15th March
item.description:
Blocking the pavement

item.title: Influx of graffiti along Stockwell main road SW9. Graffiti sprayed on street boxes, electric vehicle charging stations, outside building entrances., 15th March
item.description:
I like to report an influx of graffiti along Stockwell main road SW9 as you approach Stockwell underground tube station. The graffiti is very visible its outside a residential entrances such as Blair House. Mostly its on street boxes and electric car charging stations. Unfortunately it's along the entire length of Stockwell road. The graffiti has been there approximately two weeks.

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): Stockwell Road, Stockwell, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Hole in pavement, 15th March
item.description:
Hello. There’s a hole in the doorway. It’s been like this for weeks. It hasn’t been repaired or properly fenced it off.

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): Greenland Quay, Rotherhithe, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Next to the raised bed 🛏️ n the small square at the junction of Tennyson road & Priory park road, 15th March
item.description:
Black bags + loose items of clothes bags a holdall cat station & unit of drawers Dumped at both ends of the raised bed. Usually move altogether at edge of square but on way somewhere this morning so left where it was Also just noticed more rubbish at base if tree next to bikes

Nearest address to the pin placed on the map (from OS Places API): Priory Park Road, London
Report on FixMyStreet
item.category: Fly-tip Large - More than a van load

item.title: Outside Cranley Buildings in Brooke’s Market, 15th March
item.description:
Domestic sewage from the building has been overflowing onto the pavement for over a month. The area smells strongly and appears unhygienic, with wastewater running into the public drain. I have contacted Thames Water, and they confirmed that the pipework in question is the responsibility of the building owner rather than the water authority. As the issue has persisted for some time and is affecting the public pavement, I would be grateful if the Council could investigate the matter and arrange for the necessary repairs or enforcement action.
Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): 16, Dorrington Street, Holborn, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Next to kilburn grange school under enamel panel, 15th March
item.description:

Nearest address to the pin placed on the map (from OS Places API): Priory Park Road, London

item.category: Fly-tip Medium - Up to medium size van load

item.title: Fly tipping.. obstructing pathway., 15th March
item.description:
Fly tipping along public pathway. Past two weeks even more multiple black sacks fly tipped.. not sure what’s inside. Building appliances left. Causing obstruction for pathway as well as my back gate.

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): 6, The Drive, Northumberland Heath, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Bags of corrugated asbestos sheeting, 15th March
item.description:
Several black plastic bags of broken corrugated asbestos are dumped beside the footpath - suggest these have been fly tipped in the last 6 months.

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): Hillcrest Road, Dartford

item.title: Public bin, 15th March
item.description:
I've reported this last week and it'd still NOT BEEN DONE NOT GOOD ENOUGH THIS PUBLIC BIN IS AT THE DIDE OF MY HOUSE SUSING MOREVPROBLEMS LIKE MICE RATS AND BLACK BAGS BEEN PUT THERE TOO CAN YOUNPLEASE GET THIS DONE AND NOR WAIT FOR THE NEXT TIME AS NO WORK COLLEGE has been near this in over 3 weeks now I've sentvthe Councillor a email on this problem too so ROYAL BOROUGH OF GREENWICH PULL YOUR FINGERCOUT AND GET THIS DONE AS WE ALL PAY COUNCIL TAX AND RENT WHICH IS GOING UP AND GETTING NOTHING BACK YOUR A DISGRACE
Nearest address to the pin placed on the map (from OS Places API): Kempt Street, Woolwich
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Dumped Items (Again) On Seal Street, 15th March
item.description:
Items have been dumped at the end of Seal Street again below the no dumping sign. This is a constant problem in this location.

Nearest address to the pin placed on the map (from OS Places API): April Street, Hackney, London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Deep pothole (roughly 7 inches deep) On the crossing/speedbump outside Woodknoll Drive, 15th March
item.description:
This pothole has been there for a few weeks from what i’ve noticed as i don’t drive down the road often. But It’s a relatively blind corner and you wouldn’t expect there to be a pothole that side on the side of a speedbump. It seems dangerous as it’s also a crossing and could cause harm to anyone partially sighted who may not see the pothole.
Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): Chislehurst Road, Bickley, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Long stretch of potholes from Perry Road leading into Nazeing., 15th March
item.description:
Far too dangerous to stop and photograph as so many on top of each other they all filled with water so are extremely dangerous. People have put cones as so many tired been damaged. Holes before and after were fixed few weeks back but work men ignored these ones and told residence they only do ones reported.

Report on FixMyStreet
